batch1.rationale: P(prime in 1-20) = 8/20 — single-event probability via favorable/total counting. New basic_probability tag added 2026-05-13.

Telescoping sum 1/(2k-1)(2k+1) via partial fractions. Routed to progression (sequences/series umbrella, sum-formula scope) per <5 systemic-gap rule — telescoping cohort = 4 total. Imperfect fit: AP/GP drill cohort overlaps with series-summation practice.
Identify Y=f(X) rule from table of (X,Y) pairs. Tabular analogue of function_graph's 'identify the graph' — same identification skill, different representation.

Parity reasoning: a even ⇒ b odd via (odd)^n=odd, (even)^n=even, odd−even=odd. Parity is the core skill, not exponent computation — exponent_rules drill cohort wouldn't teach this. Flag for 3b.3 review; may justify parity_arithmetic tag if Phase 2b textbook extraction surfaces more such questions.
Remainder theorem: f(x)=x³−ax²+6x−14 divisible by (x−3) ⇒ f(3)=0 ⇒ a=31/9. Parameter-finding via remainder theorem — exact polynomial_remainder scope.

Digit-swap word problem reducing to two-variable linear system in (a,b): original−swap=18 plus multiplication-error condition. System solving is the math kernel; word_problem_arithmetic scope is mixtures/proportions, doesn't fit.
Simplify cot x + sin x/(1+cos x). Combine + apply sin²+cos²=1 + cancel ⇒ 1/sin x. Pure Pythagorean-identity simplification.
y=15/x — identify the point NOT on the hyperbola via xy=15 test. Point-on-graph verification, not coefficient computation.

Tolerance interval x=8.7±0.4 ⇒ x∈[8.3,9.1], pick values inside. Force-fit per refined rule: number_representation drill cohort (rounding/approximation/comparing reals) does teach tolerance-band reasoning. Near-neighbor fit, not exact.
Compute A+B element-wise for two 2×2 matrices with variable entries. No determinant, no inverse — matrix_operation by definition.
2x²−3x−5 < x²−1 ⇒ x²−3x−4<0 ⇒ (x−4)(x+1)<0 ⇒ −1<x<4; integer enumeration from given set; sum=6. Quadratic-inequality solve dominates.

Identify which of three named functions are decreasing on [−10,−3]. Qualitative monotonicity per function family — no derivative computation expected. function_graph (function-behavior identification) over derivative_application.
P(composite in 1-20). Single-event probability via favorable/total counting.
Telescoping sum 1/(2k)(2k+2). Same telescoping technique as Q1A-Q18; same force-fit rationale.
System: x²+5x+6<0 (gives −3<x<−2) AND 4x+3≥3x+½ (gives x≥−2.5). Intersection [−2.5,−2). Quadratic inequality is the binding skill.
Variant of Q1A-Q21: identify Y=f(X) from table of pairs. Tabular identification.
Variant of Q1A-Q22: parity reasoning with different powers. Same flag rationale — parity is the core skill, not exponent computation.
Variant of Q1A-Q23: f(x)=x³+ax²+7x−18 divisible by (x+3) ⇒ f(−3)=0 ⇒ a=22/3. Remainder theorem with parameter.
Variant of Q1A-Q25: digit-swap with multiplication-error + sum conditions. Two-variable linear system after setup.
Simplify tan x + cos x/(1+sin x) ⇒ 1/cos x. Symmetric variant of Q1A-Q26.
y=−12/x — identify point NOT on hyperbola via xy=−12. Variant of Q1A-Q30.

Tolerance interval 500±12.3 ⇒ [487.7,512.3]. CSV topic is statistics (subtopic 'алдаа'/error) but the math is approximation/tolerance, not statistical dispersion (variance/SD). Same near-neighbor concern as Q1A-Q31.
Compute A−B element-wise. Variant of Q1A-Q32.
3n²−10n+2 < 5n+2 ⇒ 3n²−15n<0 ⇒ 3n(n−5)<0 ⇒ 0<n<5; integer enumeration; sum=10. Quadratic-inequality solve.
Variant of Q1A-Q36: identify which of y=6/x, (1-x)², √(x-3) increase on [5,8]. Qualitative monotonicity identification.

batch2.rationale: f(x)=2x^(-1/2), f'(4)=-1/8. Power-rule on radical function.
f(x)=4/√x, f'(9)=-2/27. Power-rule on radical function.
Variant. Power-rule derivative.
Variant. Power-rule derivative.
Compare numbers vs √2≈1.414. 'Comparing irrationals' explicit in number_representation scope.
IQR of 9 scores = 25. IQR explicit in dispersion scope; data_display is for chart-reading.
√((a-1)²)+√((a-4)²) under 1<a<4 → 3. Force-fit: drill cohort teaches √(x²)=|x|, even though deeper skill is abs-value-with-interval (no tag).
Compute M^(-1) for 2×2. det=2 then formula.
P(x) divided by (x+1), P(-1)=-5. Remainder theorem.
x+y+z=7 natural solutions via stars-and-bars: C(6,2)=15. Stars-and-bars explicit in scope.
Build polynomial from roots: (x+1)(x-1)(x-3) expansion.
|2x-1|+|x+5|=10 via case analysis; each case is linear. No abs-value-equation tag; force-fit. SURVEILLANCE: abs-value equation pattern (1 of 5 needed).
dy/dx=2x-3 with initial condition. Antiderivative + constant via point.
Normal line at x=1 for x²-4x+5. Tangent/normal scope.
Section formula in triangle, vector representation of internal point.
Multi-step rational simplification. CSV 'word problem' rationale was wrong.
Cube cross-sectioned into two prisms; volume ratio.
Reflection across line y=3x+1 — non-axis reflection.
Parameter problem via discriminant<0 → quadratic inequality in a.
P(A')=1-P(A)=1/4. Complement of single event.
Distance from point to origin via formula.
Matrix dimensional conformability check.
sin α=2/3 in Q2, find cos α=-√5/3. Exact-value computation with quadrant sign.
3^(-2)=1/9. Negative-exponent rule.
a/2=b/3 substitution into (3a-b)/(a+2b). Algebraic substitution + simplification (not a word problem).

batch3.rationale: Radical equation with domain check. Force-fit; partial transfer.
Numerical eval via factoring recognition. Hybrid with rational_expression.
Parameter problem on integer solutions of quadratic inequality.
|2x+3|>|4-x| via squaring. Abs-value inequality in scope.
Mean from grouped frequency, solve for missing frequency.
Cayley-Hamilton: A²=2A-E ⇒ A³=3A-2E. matrix_operation as umbrella; rare topic.
Matching exponent/log values. Force-fit; SURVEILLANCE for multi-skill matching pattern.
Division by (x+3)² remainder via P(-3) and P'(-3).

batch4.rationale: Infer coefficients from one graph, match another.

batch5.rationale: Solve linear system via given A^(-1).

batch6.rationale: 3D parametric line + plane intersection. Force-fit; surveillance 1/5.
Count 3-digit perfect cubes: 5³=125 through 9³=729 ⇒ 5 numbers. Reclassified from number_representation to number_theory (new tag added 2026-05-13).

batch7.rationale: Count 3-digit perfect squares: 10² through 31² ⇒ 22 numbers. Reclassified from number_representation to number_theory.

batch8.rationale: Product=864, GCD=6 ⇒ coprime pair mn=24, (m,n)=(3,8), numbers (18,48). GCD/LCM pair enumeration. Reclassified from number_representation.

batch9.rationale: (2n²+n+11)/(n+2) natural ⇒ (n+2)|17 ⇒ n=15. Integer-divisibility reasoning is the binding skill (polynomial division is the technique to expose it). Reclassified from polynomial_remainder to number_theory per new tag scope: 'distinct from polynomial_remainder (polynomial not integer divisibility)'.
Product=4800, GCD=20 ⇒ coprime pair mn=12, (m,n)=(3,4), numbers (60,80). Reclassified from number_representation.

batch10.rationale: (a+3)/2-1/a=2 → a²-a-2=0; both a=2 and a=-1 give 1/5.
(3n²+7n-11)/(n+2) natural ⇒ (n+2)|13 ⇒ n=11. Integer-divisibility reasoning. number_theory (new tag).
x²+28/x²=4√7; substitute u=x²; double root, product of x values = -2√7.
Pigeonhole: 6 colors, draw 7 to guarantee pair.
Line tangent to parabola: discriminant=0, m=-2 or 6; m₁²+m₂²=40.
Quartic factored to n(n-1)(n+1)(n-6)<0; integers in (1,6) count=4.
Leading term identification from graph end-behavior.
Horizontal asymptote of sum of rational functions: slants cancel, constants=2.

batch10_rest.rationale: k! divisible by 100 requires ≥2 factors of 5. Count=9 for k=1..9. Factorial divisibility.

rows_276_300.rationale: Binomial expansion x-free term. Force-fit. Surveillance 1/5.

rows_301_325.rationale: Rational simplify w/ diff of squares.
